what_it_does: Defines a UI contract entry for the claude-memory resource, documenting the filesystem data model Claude Code uses for persistent instructions: the CLAUDE.md scope hierarchy (managed-policy, user, project, local, subdir), the auto-memory directory structure (MEMORY.md index plus topic files), the writer split, a stateless state model, three planned operations (list, update, act), error modes, and links to related resources, while repeatedly noting that no company endpoint exists today and naming the filesystem-only interim path.
observations: Contract-entry markdown with YAML frontmatter (type, resource, summary, status=planned, relates-to).
Describes two complementary systems: operator-written CLAUDE.md loaded in full and Claude-written auto-memory loaded via a capped MEMORY.md index plus on-demand topic files.
CLAUDE.md scopes have a fixed load order (managed-policy -> user -> project -> local) and a subdir scope that loads on demand.
Includes JSON schema blocks for claude-md-file and auto-memory-dir under contract:schema markers.
Auto-memory default dir is ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/; MEMORY.md loads only the first 200 lines or 25 KB, whichever comes first.
Defines three planned outbound operations: claude-memory.list, claude-memory.update, and claude-memory.act, each with tasks, bindings, and verification notes.

Every operation's verification section names a no-company-endpoint gap and calls it an F10.1 gap-adoption candidate; the interim path is direct filesystem read/edit or the built-in /memory slash command.
State model is explicitly stateless: no lifecycle FSM, only a session-start load transition.
Caller distinction is based on writer: operator owns CLAUDE.md across all scopes; Claude Code owns auto-memory.
Includes a reality table measured on Tim's install, showing MEMORY.md at 25,829 bytes (over the 25 KB cap) and 113 topic files.
Links section disambiguates instruction-memory from transcript corpus and knowledge-corpus.
Surfaces import and rules mechanisms (@path imports, .claude/rules/ path-scoped rules, claudeMdExcludes).
standouts: Unusually explicit about missing surface: 'NO company endpoint' and 'F10.1 gap-adoption candidate' appear repeatedly.
Clear writer split: operator-written CLAUDE.md vs Claude-written auto-memory.
Live reality table showing the MEMORY.md truncation cap is currently exceeded.
Stateless model with no live reload and no opaque IDs — path is identity.
conventions: Uses contract-entry frontmatter and contract:schema / contract:op fenced blocks.
Operations follow a fixed template: op, resource, kind, status, direction, atlas, tasks, bindings, liveness, consequences, verification.
Status 'planned' is applied consistently to the resource and all operations.
Includes verification notes that distinguish verified absence from unverified gaps.
Plain markdown tables capture measured reality and property mappings.
concerns: The file claims a grep verified no endpoint, yet operation verification states are 'unverified' — a minor internal tension.
The subdir scope is in the schema but omitted from the load-order arrow in the prose.
Atlas codes (CC-23.3, CC-23.4, CC-23.5) are referenced without definition in this file.
The auto-memory project segment derivation is described as repo-root-based but the example path looks cwd-derived, creating ambiguity.
The managed-policy scope and paths may be aspirational; the file does not clarify current availability.
notes: A planned UI contract for Claude Code's local memory that is unusually candid about the missing company endpoint and the filesystem-only interim path.
questions: What do the atlas codes CC-23.3, CC-23.4, CC-23.5 map to?
Is the managed-policy scope implemented today or only planned?
How exactly is the <project> segment of the auto-memory directory derived (repo root vs cwd)?
What is the F10.1 gap-adoption process and who owns it?
How should a UI visually distinguish operator-written CLAUDE.md from Claude-written auto-memory?
Are .claude/rules/ files also considered CLAUDE.md-equivalent for load order or a separate category?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to contract the data model a UI renders and edits for Claude Code memory; the file fulfills that while also documenting the planned-vs-implemented gap, so purpose matches actual.
apparent_themes: Honest gap documentation and naming of missing endpoints.
Filesystem-as-identity: no opaque IDs, path is the address.
Strict separation of operator-written instructions from agent-written memory.
Scope hierarchy and load order as the primary context-composition mechanism.
Trust, consent, and machine-local boundaries for auto-memory.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to give a UI a precise, adoptable contract for mirroring Claude Code's /memory behavior before a programmatic company endpoint exists, and to make the absence of that endpoint unambiguous so integration work can target the right gaps.
novelty: The combination of a formal contract schema with repeated 'no company endpoint' gap naming, a measured user-install reality table, and an honest filesystem-only interim path is unusual for a resource spec.
contribution: A scoped vocabulary and data model for Claude Code memory that separates CLAUDE.md from auto-memory, defines scope load order and writer ownership, and records the missing endpoint gap so a fused system can preserve the contract and adopt the right surface later.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a planned UI contract entry that defines the filesystem data model, scope hierarchy, operations, and known implementation gaps for Claude Code's persistent memory (CLAUDE.md and auto-memory).
intention_for_embedding: It was written to provide a UI with a neutral, precise contract for rendering and editing Claude Code memory artifacts while explicitly documenting that no company endpoint exists today.
suggested_fields: gap_adoption_candidate — to capture F10.1 and similar named gaps.
writer — to tag each memory artifact as operator or Claude.
load_limit_status — to record whether an index is at or over its cap.
proposed_edge_kinds: path-scopes — a rule's paths frontmatter scopes it to file globs.
indexes — MEMORY.md points to topic files.
loads-after — broader CLAUDE.md scopes load before narrower ones.
trust-gates — workspace trust gates project-scope overrides.
